cards.definition: The strength of the lime-wash solution used to protect young dadap shade trees from sun scorch before they're established.
cards.analogy: Sunscreen for a young tree, mixed to a specific strength rather than 'a splash of lime in some water.'
cards.why_it_matters: A specific, checkable ratio — worth knowing before assuming any whitish coating on young dadap stems is this treatment.
cards.cloze: Young dadap plants are protected from scorching sun using a lime solution made from ___ kgs of spray lime in 100 litres of water.

cards.definition: The minimum rainfall that should have fallen before pruning begins, with exceptions for high-elevation or irrigated blocks.
cards.analogy: Waiting for the ground and plant to actually be in growing condition before cutting back, not pruning on the calendar alone.
cards.why_it_matters: Pruning before this threshold, outside the stated exceptions, risks stressing plants that aren't yet in active growth.
cards.cloze: Pruning should not start until a minimum of ___ inches of rain has been received (except above 3500 ft or in irrigated blocks).

cards.definition: The rainfall threshold that should be reached before shade trees are lopped or regulated.
cards.analogy: A different, higher rain threshold than pruning's — shade work waits for a more established wet season before starting.
cards.why_it_matters: Confusing this with the lower pruning-rain threshold would mean lopping shade too early in the season.
cards.cloze: Shade lopping/regulation should preferably be done only after receiving ___ inches of rainfall.

cards.definition: The breadth of a cradle pit — a soil-and-moisture-conservation trench dug between coffee rows — alongside its depth and renovation schedule.
cards.analogy: A maintained trench, not a dig-once-and-forget structure — it needs periodic renovation to keep working.
cards.why_it_matters: A pit built to a different, smaller breadth than this spec likely won't hold or conserve moisture as intended.
cards.cloze: Cradle pits are opened with ___ inch breadth and 12 inch depth, renovated every 2-3 years.

cards.definition: The spacing between dadap shade trees, planted to provide temporary lower-canopy shade while permanent shade trees establish.
cards.analogy: A temporary scaffold of shade, spaced to cover the canopy gap until longer-term shade trees take over.
cards.why_it_matters: Spaced too far apart, dadap fails at the one job it's planted for — filling the shade gap early on.
cards.cloze: Dadap plants are spaced about ___ feet apart to provide temporary lower canopy shade.

cards.definition: The height range for dadap stakes at planting, alongside their minimum girth requirement.
cards.analogy: A stake substantial enough to survive being planted directly and take root, not a thin cutting.
cards.why_it_matters: A stake shorter or thinner than this spec has a real chance of simply failing to establish.
cards.cloze: Dadap stakes used for planting should be ___ metre(s) in height with a minimum girth of 5-6 cm.
